pytest-patterns.d: Comprehensive pytest skill covering modern patterns for Python test automation including fixtures, parametrize, async testing, mocking, coverage strategies, integration tests, and conftest organisation for pytest 7.0+ projects.

design-extract.d: Extracts complete design systems, Tailwind configs, and Figma variables from any live URL.

agent-skills.d: Production-grade engineering command suite for AI coding agents spanning the full development lifecycle — spec, plan, build, test, review, web performance, ship, and simplify.

code-review-and-quality.d: Code review and quality enforcement workflow checking code style and patterns.

code-simplification.d: Code simplification workflow identifying opportunities to reduce cognitive complexity.

incremental-implementation.d: Incremental implementation workflow prioritizing execution of planned steps systematically.

performance-optimization.d: Measurement-driven performance workflow: baseline with Lighthouse and RUM, identify bottlenecks via profiling, fix targeted issues (N+1 queries, render blocking, unoptimized images), verify against Core Web Vitals thresholds (LCP ≤2.5s, INP ≤200ms, CLS ≤0.1),

planning-and-task-breakdown.d: Planning and task breakdown workflow decomposing features into manageable vertical slices.

shipping-and-launch.d: Shipping and launch readiness checks for code deployment and integration.

spec-driven-development.d: Spec-driven development workflow enforcing specification generation before any coding starts.

test-driven-development.d: Forces the AI agent to follow a strict red-green-refactor TDD workflow — explicitly blocking horizontal slicing (all tests first, then all code). Tests verify behaviour through public interfaces only, blocking code generation that skips the test step, and enfo

pptx.d: Extracts slide content from PowerPoint (.pptx) files using markitdown, applies edits or design principles in-place, and repacks the file — enabling agents to read, modify, and write structured presentation files without a GUI.

skill-creator.d: Interviews the user through a structured dialogue to elicit the skill's purpose, trigger conditions, and step-by-step instructions, then programmatically writes a new SKILL.md file ready for use in a Claude Code or Codex CLI skills directory.

claude-video.d: Enables AI agents to watch videos by downloading them, extracting frames, and transcribing audio for multimodal analysis.

browser-harness.d: Self-healing harness for direct browser control via CDP, enabling agents to write custom helpers at runtime.

stagehand.d: SDK for browser agents that combines deterministic browser automation with AI-assisted page interaction.

evidence-attestation.d: Produces privacy-safe proof objects from agent claims by recording what evidence supports or contradicts each claim, what raw content was intentionally omitted, what verdict follows, and what would make the evidence stale.

plan-decompose-gh-wallfacer.d: Chat, specs, tasks, and code. An autonomous engineering platform.

autonomous-swe.d: Autonomous software engineering agent capable of end-to-end debugging, code generation, and self-correction across complex multi-file codebases.

attendees.d: Count attendees for any event by checking Favor Event Tickets first, then Fluro as a fallback.

count-checkin-by-church.d: Build or refresh a NORMALIZED per-church check-in count.

export-attendees.d: Sync and export attendee data from Favor Event Tickets into a Google Sheets masterlist.

financial-assistance.d: Process financial assistance applications - coupon assignment, request tracking, and updates.

setup-matt-pocock-skills.d: Scaffolds per-repo configuration for other engineering skills (like triage, tdd, diagnose).

setup-pre-commit.d: Set up Husky pre-commit hooks with lint-staged, Prettier, type checking, and tests.

skills.d: The ultimate capstone suite encompassing all of Matt Pocock's engineering, productivity, and personal skills.

tdd.d: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor, verifying behavior through public interfaces.

teach.d: Teach the user a new skill or concept in a workspace with mission, reference materials, and lessons.

to-issues.d: Breaks a plan, spec, or PRD into independently-grabbable GitHub issues as tracer-bullet vertical slices that each cut through all integration layers end-to-end. Classifies each slice HITL or AFK, maps dependency chains, quizzes the user on granularity, and pub

to-prd.d: Synthesises the current conversation context and codebase knowledge into a fully-structured PRD — problem statement, extensive numbered user stories, implementation decisions (modules, interfaces, schema), testing decisions, and out-of-scope items — then publi

triage.d: Moves GitHub issues through a two-category (bug/enhancement) × five-state (needs-triage/needs-info/ready-for-agent/ready-for-human/wontfix) state machine. Reproduces bugs from issue steps, runs a domain-aware grilling session when needed, writes structured age

ubiquitous-language.d: Extracts and formalises a project's domain terminology into a shared glossary, enforcing consistent naming across code and conversations to eliminate ambiguity. Removed from mattpocock/skills suite in v1.0.1.

write-a-skill.d: Guides creation of new agent skills through a structured requirements interview, then produces a SKILL.md with a trigger-aware description, progressive-disclosure layout, and optional bundled scripts or reference files — ready for installation in any Claude Co

writing-great-skills.d: Reference for writing and editing skills to be predictable — vocabulary and principles for deterministic skills.

zoom-out.d: Signals the agent to ascend one layer of abstraction and produce a map of all relevant modules, callers, and domain-glossary terms in the unfamiliar code area, without explaining implementation details. Removed from mattpocock/skills suite in v1.0.1.

gaia-audit.d: Performs a focused source-level correction for one target registry entry — verifying links, checking evidence classes, and filing an inline-diff fix PR with full citations.

gaia-bot-curate.d: Runs an automated batch curation pass over the Gaia skill registry — scanning for new agent skills, validating evidence, and opening versioned draft PRs without human intervention.

gaia-curate.d: Expands the Gaia skill registry with new, fully evidenced AI agent skills — researching skill sources, running validation, opening versioned PRs, and appending discovered marketplaces to the sources registry in one end-to-end workflow.

gaia-curation-review.d: Reviews pending skill submissions against registry standards — checking evidence class thresholds, naming conventions, and tier accuracy before approving or requesting revisions.

gaia-docs-sync.d: Keeps the generated Gaia documentation site in sync with the registry — rebuilding HTML pages, updating skill indexes, and regenerating badges when registry content changes.

gaia-draft-curate.d: Creates structured draft skill entries for registry review — staging new discoveries with placeholder evidence and flagging fields that need human validation before promotion.

gaia-integrity.d: Validates the structural integrity of the Gaia registry — checking schema compliance, detecting duplicate IDs, verifying cross-references, and reporting any inconsistencies that would break build or generation.

gaia-meta-audit.d: Produces a prioritized review queue of Gaia registry entries needing attention — flagging stale evidence, broken links, mis-classified tiers, and naming inconsistencies in one structured audit pass.

gaia-preview.d: Generates a preview render of proposed registry changes — showing how new or modified skill entries will appear on the profile page and in the skill graph before the PR is merged.

gaia-wiki-sync.d: Synchronizes the Gaia project wiki with the current registry state — updating skill pages, contributor profiles, and changelog entries to reflect the latest approved changes.

graphify-triage.d: Analyzes the Gaia skill dependency graph to surface orphaned nodes, missing prerequisites, and structural inconsistencies — producing a prioritized list of graph fixes needed.

open-design.d: Local-first design engine for generating high-fidelity UI/UX prototypes and brand assets from structured intent.

feed-monitoring.d: Continuous monitoring of content feeds with intelligent discovery and state tracking.

brainstorming.d: Explores user intent, requirements, and design before implementation. Turns ideas into fully formed designs and specs through natural collaborative dialogue.

dispatching-parallel-agents.d: Delegates independent tasks to specialized agents with isolated context to work concurrently, dramatically reducing wall-clock time on multi-part problems.

executing-plans.d: Loads a written implementation plan, reviews it critically, executes all tasks sequentially, and reports when complete — without improvising beyond the plan's scope.

finishing-a-development-branch.d: Guides completion of development work by verifying tests first, then presenting structured options for merge, PR creation, or cleanup — never declaring done without a passing build.

receiving-code-review.d: Processes code review feedback with technical rigor and verification before implementation — questioning unclear feedback and confirming fixes with tests rather than blindly applying suggestions.

requesting-code-review.d: Dispatches a code reviewer subagent with isolated context to catch issues before they cascade — producing a structured review report that guides targeted fixes.

subagent-driven-development.d: Executes plans by dispatching a fresh subagent per task with a two-stage review (spec compliance then code quality), keeping the orchestrator context clean throughout.

superpowers.d: A complete software development methodology for coding agents — brainstorming, planning, parallel execution, systematic debugging, code review loops, and branch discipline unified into a single agentic workflow.

systematic-debugging.d: Finds the root cause before attempting any fix — building a minimal reproduction, forming ranked hypotheses, and instrumenting to confirm before writing a single corrective line.

using-git-worktrees.d: Ensures every piece of feature work begins in an isolated workspace — creating a native git worktree when available, falling back to tool-provided isolation otherwise.

verification-before-completion.d: Requires running verification commands and confirming their output before claiming any work is complete, fixed, or passing — no claim without evidence.

writing-plans.d: Writes comprehensive implementation plans that break multi-step tasks into bite-sized, independently executable sub-tasks before any code is touched.

few-shot-learning.d: Conditions the model on a small set of examples within the prompt to adapt to new tasks without weight updates.

self-consistency.d: Samples multiple reasoning paths and selects the most consistent answer by majority vote.

impeccable.d: Elite design vocabulary and audit tool for polishing AI-generated frontend code.

sentiment-analysis.d: Classifies the affective polarity (positive / negative / neutral, or fine-grained) of user-generated text. Covers the full pipeline from raw noisy input through preprocessing, inference, and output normalisation. Stack is intentionally tool-agnostic — three im

implement-with-discernment.d: Applies deliberate judgment before writing code: evaluating whether to implement at all, scoping to the minimum needed, deferring or rejecting features that add complexity without proportionate value.

agentdb-advanced.d: Implements sub-millisecond cross-node vector synchronization using QUIC protocol with hybrid metadata-filtered search and MMR diversity retrieval.

agentdb-learning.d: Builds self-improving agent memory by analyzing task success patterns and adapting retrieval strategies with AgentDB-backed vector persistence.

agentdb-memory-patterns.d: Designs recurring memory storage patterns for AI agents with LRU caching, SQLite persistence, and associative retrieval across multiple memory types.

agentdb-optimization.d: Tunes AgentDB vector indices, implements database sharding, and monitors production performance for large-scale distributed agent memory.

agentdb-vector-search.d: Performs semantic similarity search over high-dimensional embeddings using cosine, Euclidean, dot-product, or custom distance metrics with HNSW indexing.

agentdb.d: Complete AgentDB vector memory platform fused from 5 discipline skills — QUIC-synchronized distributed storage, pattern learning, memory design, optimization, and vector search.

agentic-jujutsu.d: Analyzes git diffs for complexity, churn, and risk scores to prioritize review attention and flag dangerous changes.

browser.d: Playwright-based browser automation for web scraping, E2E testing, form interaction, and screenshot capture within agent workflows.

dual-collect.d: Collects and aggregates results from headless Codex workers stored in shared memory with filtering and health status reporting.

dual-coordinate.d: Coordinates hybrid Claude+Codex workflows by routing tasks between interactive reasoning phases and parallel background execution.

dual-mode.d: Fuses headless worker spawning, result collection, and hybrid workflow coordination into a complete Claude+Codex parallel orchestration pattern.

dual-spawn.d: Spawns headless Codex workers from Claude Code for parallel background execution with configurable worker types and shared memory.

flow-nexus-neural.d: Trains neural networks across distributed E2B sandbox clusters with support for feedforward, LSTM, GAN, autoencoder, and transformer architectures, federated learning, and a model marketplace.

flow-nexus-platform.d: Full lifecycle management of the Flow Nexus cloud AI platform: user authentication, sandbox environments, app store deployment, payment processing, and challenge systems with Queen Seraphina AI assistant.

flow-nexus-swarm.d: Cloud-based AI swarm orchestration platform supporting hierarchical, mesh, ring, and star topologies with event-driven workflows, message queue processing, and intelligent agent assignment.

flow-nexus.d: Complete Flow Nexus platform: multi-topology swarm deployment, cloud platform management with Queen Seraphina AI assistant, and distributed neural training.

github-code-review.d: Automates GitHub pull request code review workflows including diff analysis, inline comments, review assignments, and approval gating.

github-multi-repo.d: Manages synchronized operations across multiple GitHub repositories including cross-repo PRs, dependency tracking, and bulk workflow automation.

github-project-management.d: Manages GitHub Projects boards, milestones, issue tracking, and sprint planning through automated workflow integration.

github-release-management.d: Automates GitHub release creation, changelog generation, semantic versioning, and release note publishing.

github-suite.d: Full GitHub platform automation fused from 5 skills — code review, multi-repo coordination, project management, release management, and workflow automation.

github-workflow-automation.d: Designs and manages GitHub Actions workflows for CI/CD automation, scheduled tasks, and event-driven agent triggers.

hive-mind-coordination.d: Queen-led collective intelligence with Byzantine, majority, and weighted consensus mechanisms, eight worker specializations, and persistent collective SQLite memory with LRU caching.

hooks-automation.d: Designs agent lifecycle hooks and timer-based background tasks for automated quality gates and scheduled workflows.

pair-programming.d: Structures collaborative coding sessions between a primary implementation agent and a review subagent with continuous feedback loops.

performance-analysis.d: Profiles agent and system execution for CPU, memory, and I/O hotspots and produces actionable optimization recommendations.

reasoningbank-agentdb.d: Persists agent reasoning patterns in AgentDB vector memory and retrieves them semantically for continuous self-improvement across sessions.

reasoningbank-intelligence.d: Implements adaptive learning through pattern recognition, strategy optimization, and meta-learning that improves agent decision quality from cumulative experience.

reasoningbank.d: Fuses adaptive pattern learning with persistent vector memory to build a self-improving agent knowledge base across sessions.

ruflo-v3.d: Complete Ruflo v3 modernization sprint: CLI modernization, core implementation, DDD architecture, MCP optimization, memory unification, performance tuning, security overhaul, and swarm coordination.

ruflo.d: The complete Ruflo orchestration platform: flow nexus, AgentDB memory sovereignty, GitHub operations, hive-mind consensus, reasoning bank, and v3 modernization — unified at 6★ apex.

skill-builder.d: Guides creation of new Ruflo skills through templating, testing, and publishing workflows.

sparc-methodology.d: Systematic 5-phase AI development methodology (Specification, Pseudocode, Architecture, Refinement, Completion) with 17 agent modes.

stream-chain.d: Chains agent outputs sequentially so each step receives prior output as context, enabling multi-stage data transformation pipelines.

swarm-advanced.d: Domain-specific swarm orchestration patterns for research, development, testing, and analysis workflows with neural learning and cross-session state persistence.

swarm-orchestration.d: Initializes and manages multi-agent swarm network topologies (hierarchical, mesh, ring, star) with automatic load balancing, fault tolerance, and shared memory coordination.

v3-cli-modernization.d: Refactors the Ruflo CLI for improved UX, plugin architecture, extensibility, and modern command-line conventions.

v3-core-implementation.d: Implements foundational Ruflo v3 platform architecture including plugin discovery, server lifecycle management, and API contract definitions.

v3-ddd-architecture.d: Applies Domain-Driven Design principles to the Ruflo v3 architecture including bounded contexts, aggregate roots, domain events, and ubiquitous language.

v3-integration-deep.d: Connects Ruflo v3 subsystems via shared contracts, event buses, and compatibility layers for coherent cross-component operation.

v3-mcp-optimization.d: Optimizes Ruflo v3 MCP server performance through connection pooling, request batching, tool schema caching, and latency reduction strategies.

v3-memory-unification.d: Unifies disparate Ruflo v3 memory subsystems (AgentDB, RVF, RAG memory) into a single coherent memory management layer.

v3-performance-optimization.d: Profiles and optimizes Ruflo v3 platform performance across startup time, request latency, memory footprint, and throughput.

v3-security-overhaul.d: Comprehensive Ruflo v3 security overhaul: zero-trust federation, PII detection, mTLS/ed25519 authentication, and CVE scanning.

v3-swarm-coordination.d: Implements Ruflo v3 hierarchical-mesh hybrid swarm topology with anti-drift mechanisms and SONA neural pattern learning.

verification-quality.d: Implements structured pre-completion verification checklists ensuring quality gates are met before task finalization.

worker-benchmarks.d: Benchmarks Ruflo background worker performance across latency, throughput, memory usage, and quality score dimensions.

worker-integration.d: Maps trigger events to optimal agent combinations for background task execution with performance tracking and adaptive feedback.

graphify.d: Maps codebases and documentation into a queryable knowledge graph using AST analysis and semantic extraction.

career-ops.d: Agentic job search pipeline that automates scanning, fit scoring, and CV tailoring.

ai-dev-jobs-mcp.d: MCP tool for finding AI dev jobs.

mcp-builder.d: A community implementation of the MCP builder skill from the antigravity-awesome-skills repository, capable of creating Model Context Protocol servers in Python and Node.js.

n8n-mcp-tools-expert.d: Expert at integrating n8n with MCP (Model Context Protocol). Build n8n workflows that expose MCP tools, or use MCP tools within n8n workflows.

readme-generate.d: Scans a Java Spring project for controller annotations, extracts REST API endpoint definitions, and automatically generates structured API documentation in README format.

dspy.d: Declarative programming of language model pipelines, automatically optimizing prompts and RAG retrieval using bootstrapping and teleprompters.

unittest-generator.d: Autonomous Claude agent that generates comprehensive unittest.TestCase suites from source code, organising tests into concept-based subfolders under a tests/ directory with proper imports, fixtures, and edge-case coverage.

find-skills.d: Searches the skills.sh registry by keyword or category, queries install counts to surface popular skills, and auto-installs the selected skill into the current project's skills directory.

hermes-tweet.d: Installable Hermes Agent skill and plugin for X/Twitter search, reply reading, user lookup, monitoring, follower export, and approval-gated write actions.

orchestkit-rag.d: Production-grade RAG retrieval skill covering 30+ patterns including core pipeline composition, HyDE query expansion, pgvector hybrid search, cross-encoder reranking, multimodal chunking, and agentic self-RAG and corrective-RAG loops.

mcp-tool-developer.d: Build Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers and tools from scratch. Full-stack MCP development with TypeScript/Python, testing, deployment, and registry publishing.
